question_text: A metal block is heated from 20°C to 80°C, causing it to expand. If the block's volume increases by 2%, which statement best explains what happens to the density of the metal block?

options.label: A
options.text: The density increases because the block gets hotter

options.label: B
options.text: The density decreases because the mass stays the same but the volume increases

options.label: C
options.text: The density remains unchanged because density does not depend on temperature

options.label: D
options.text: The density increases because the particles move faster when heated

explanation: When a material is heated, it expands but its mass stays the same. Since density = mass ÷ volume, and the volume increases while mass remains constant, the density must decrease. This is why hot materials are less dense than cold materials.
